prompt: Which control sequence best supports evening circadian-friendly lighting in a corporate office?

options.text: Gradually reduce melanopic content and illuminance after sunset while maintaining safe egress lighting
Switch all zones to maximum 6500 K at 10 p.m.
Turn off all lighting including egress paths
Disable occupancy sensors permanently

explanation: Evening lighting transitions toward lower circadian stimulus while maintaining safety lighting, aligning with WELL Light day-night contrast strategies.

distractorExplanations.b: High cool light late at night increases circadian disruption.
distractorExplanations.c: Egress lighting must remain for life safety.
distractorExplanations.d: Occupancy-based control helps save energy and tailor lighting to use.

prompt: A deep-plan office uses light shelves on the perimeter. What is their primary daylighting function?

options.text: Reflect daylight deeper into the space while shading direct sun near the window
Increase structural wind load on the curtain wall
Filter drinking water at the facade
Measure CO₂ in the return air stream

explanation: Light shelves redirect daylight toward the ceiling to illuminate interior zones and reduce direct glare at the window wall.

distractorExplanations.b: Wind load may change slightly but is not the primary design intent.
distractorExplanations.c: Water treatment is unrelated to architectural daylight devices.
distractorExplanations.d: CO₂ sensing is an Air/I AQ strategy, not a light shelf function.

prompt: Which WELL Thermal Comfort strategy helps address individual differences in preferred temperature?

options.text: Provide adjustable diffusers, fans, or zoning where feasible within acceptable environmental bounds
Fix one temperature for all occupants with no local adjustment ever
Remove all thermostats from the building
Disable all windows permanently

explanation: Personal or local control within safe limits improves satisfaction when central systems cannot perfectly match every occupant's preference.

distractorExplanations.b: No single setpoint satisfies all metabolic and clothing differences.
distractorExplanations.c: Removing controls prevents corrective feedback to HVAC systems.
distractorExplanations.d: Operable windows or local control may be part of adaptive comfort solutions.

prompt: A project measures Equivalent Melanopic Lux (EML) at 9 a.m. near a window workstation. Why is time of day important for interpretation?

options.text: Circadian-effective light targets vary by time of day to support appropriate day-night entrainment
EML is only valid at midnight
Morning EML readings are always invalid for WELL
Time of day does not affect lighting metrics

explanation: WELL Light evaluates whether daytime periods provide adequate circadian stimulus relative to time-of-day expectations, not a single static threshold at all hours.

distractorExplanations.b: Melanopic metrics apply throughout the day with context-specific targets.
distractorExplanations.c: Morning can be a critical period for circadian light exposure.
distractorExplanations.d: Circadian biology depends strongly on timing of light exposure.

prompt: Which interior layout choice most increases daily walking distance for typical office workers?

options.text: Centralizing shared amenities such as printers, mail, and break areas away from every desk cluster
Placing every amenity within arm's reach of each workstation
Eliminating all corridors
Requiring all meetings by video from fixed desks only

explanation: Distributed amenities encourage short walks throughout the day, supporting WELL Movement without requiring formal exercise sessions.

distractorExplanations.b: Immediate proximity minimizes walking and sedentary behavior persists.
distractorExplanations.c: Corridors often facilitate circulation paths for movement.
distractorExplanations.d: Video-only meetings reduce incidental walking between spaces.

prompt: Drafts from an overhead supply diffuser cause local complaints despite acceptable zone air temperature. Which parameter should be evaluated first?

options.text: Air speed and diffuser throw at occupant locations
Water hardness in cooling towers only
Community event calendar
WELL exam pilot question count

explanation: Elevated local air speed can cause thermal discomfort even at acceptable dry-bulb temperatures; ASHRAE 55 includes air speed limits for comfort.

distractorExplanations.b: Cooling tower water chemistry does not explain localized drafts at desks.
distractorExplanations.c: Community programming is unrelated to diffuser throw.
distractorExplanations.d: Exam structure does not diagnose HVAC air distribution.

prompt: Which glazing property helps reduce solar heat gain while preserving daylight transmission?

options.text: Low solar heat gain coefficient (SHGC) with adequate visible light transmittance
Opaque spandrel panels on all vision areas
Single-pane clear glass with no shading on all orientations
Maximum SHGC regardless of orientation

explanation: Selecting glazing with appropriate SHGC and visible transmittance balances daylight admission with reduced radiant heat load, supporting Light and Thermal Comfort.

distractorExplanations.b: Opaque spandrel eliminates daylight in those areas.
distractorExplanations.c: Single clear glass increases heat gain and glare risk.
distractorExplanations.d: High SHGC increases cooling loads and radiant discomfort.

prompt: A WELL project includes standing meeting tables and prompts near elevators encouraging stair use. These are examples of:

options.text: Active design and behavioral prompts supporting Movement
Materials ingredient screening requirements
Sound isolation testing protocols
Portfolio aggregation rules only

explanation: Environmental cues and furnishings that reduce sedentary behavior exemplify WELL Movement through both design and operational nudges.

distractorExplanations.b: Materials focuses on product chemistry documentation.
distractorExplanations.c: Sound testing addresses acoustics, not physical activity.
distractorExplanations.d: Portfolio rules govern multi-asset certification, not stair prompts.

prompt: Which condition is most associated with 'cold floor' complaints on a slab-on-grade retail space?

options.text: Low mean radiant temperature at the floor surface despite moderate air temperature
Excessive daylight autonomy on the roof
High community engagement scores
Missing WELL AP credential on the project team

explanation: Uninsulated or thermally connected slabs can create low radiant floor temperatures, causing discomfort even when air thermostats read acceptable values.

distractorExplanations.b: Roof daylight metrics do not directly cause cold floor sensation.
distractorExplanations.c: Community metrics are unrelated to floor radiant temperature.
distractorExplanations.d: Team credentials do not change floor surface temperatures.

prompt: Emergency lighting levels differ from regular office lighting design targets. Which statement is correct?

options.text: Emergency egress illumination meets life-safety code minima and is separate from WELL circadian design goals for regular occupancy
Emergency lights must always exceed circadian EML targets at all hours
WELL prohibits any emergency lighting
Egress lighting replaces all task lighting permanently

explanation: Life-safety emergency lighting satisfies code egress requirements; WELL Light addresses regularly occupied spaces and circadian strategies under normal operations.

distractorExplanations.b: Emergency levels are not designed for circadian optimization.
distractorExplanations.c: Emergency lighting is required by code and compatible with WELL.
distractorExplanations.d: Egress lighting operates during power failure, not as daily task lighting.

prompt: Which exercise programming element aligns with WELL Movement beyond built amenities alone?

options.text: Employer-supported physical activity programs or subsidies for fitness participation
Banning all breaks during the workday
Removing all interior paths longer than 10 feet
Eliminating all windows to reduce glare

explanation: WELL Movement includes organizational policies and programs that encourage regular physical activity, not only architectural features.

distractorExplanations.b: Restricting breaks reduces opportunities for movement.
distractorExplanations.c: Shortening paths reduces incidental walking.
distractorExplanations.d: Window removal harms daylight and is unrelated to exercise programming.

prompt: A designer specifies desk task lights with adjustable intensity. Which WELL benefit is most direct?

options.text: Improved individual visual comfort and appropriate task illuminance at the workstation
Automatic Water feature compliance
Guaranteed sound isolation between offices
Elimination of thermal comfort monitoring

explanation: User-adjustable task lighting helps meet individual visual task needs and can supplement ambient circadian-designed lighting.

distractorExplanations.b: Task lights do not address water quality features.
distractorExplanations.c: Acoustic isolation requires assembly and layout strategies.
distractorExplanations.d: Thermal monitoring remains independent of desk lighting.

prompt: Which seasonal issue most commonly increases cooling demand and radiant discomfort at west-facing glazing late afternoon?

options.text: Low-angle solar heat gain through west windows
Increased waterborne pathogen testing frequency
Reduced WELL feature documentation
Lower community volunteer requirements

explanation: West-facing facades receive intense late-day sun, increasing cooling loads and radiant asymmetry; shading and glazing selection address both Light and Thermal Comfort.

distractorExplanations.b: Pathogen testing schedules do not drive solar heat gain.
distractorExplanations.c: Documentation levels do not change solar geometry.
distractorExplanations.d: Community volunteer policies are unrelated to solar orientation.

prompt: Which accessibility consideration also supports WELL Movement goals in stair design?

options.text: Compliant handrails, contrast cues, and consistent tread dimensions for safe everyday use
Steep irregular treads to discourage all users
Hidden stair locations with no signage
Locked stairs during all business hours

explanation: Safe, perceptible, code-compliant stairs encourage use by a broad population, aligning accessibility with Movement objectives.

distractorExplanations.b: Irregular treads create safety hazards and deter use.
distractorExplanations.c: Hidden stairs reduce discovery and everyday use.
distractorExplanations.d: Locked stairs block movement and egress access.

prompt: A thermal comfort survey shows dissatisfaction in a conference room after occupancy increases from 4 to 20 people. What changed most significantly?

options.text: Internal sensible heat gain and metabolic load from additional occupants
STC rating of the partition
WELL Portfolio subscription tier
Filter MERV rating in a distant AHU only

explanation: Higher occupancy increases heat generation and may exceed HVAC capacity or ventilation design, affecting thermal comfort despite unchanged thermostat setpoints.

distractorExplanations.b: Partition STC affects sound, not heat from occupants.
distractorExplanations.c: Portfolio tier does not change room occupancy heat load.
distractorExplanations.d: Distant filter rating does not explain sudden load change from people.

prompt: Which lighting quality issue is most likely when LED fixtures flicker at frequencies visible to some occupants?

options.text: Visual discomfort, headaches, or performance issues for sensitive occupants
Improved circadian entrainment for all users automatically
Higher water quality at fixtures
Reduced need for thermal comfort surveys

explanation: Flicker can cause visual stress; WELL Light emphasizes quality of illumination including controls and driver performance that minimize problematic flicker.

distractorExplanations.b: Visible flicker is generally harmful, not beneficial, to visual comfort.
distractorExplanations.c: Lighting flicker does not affect drinking water.
distractorExplanations.d: Thermal surveys address a different environmental domain.

prompt: A hybrid workforce spends only three days per week in the office. Which Movement strategy remains valuable?

options.text: Design the office to maximize active circulation and stairs for days when occupants are present
Remove all stairs because occupancy is lower
Eliminate all shared amenities to reduce walking
Disable all lighting controls on remote days

explanation: Active design still increases physical activity on in-office days; WELL Movement addresses building-mediated activity whenever occupants use the space.

distractorExplanations.b: Lower occupancy does not eliminate benefits of active design when people are present.
distractorExplanations.c: Removing amenities reduces walking opportunities.
distractorExplanations.d: Remote-day control settings are a separate operational choice from Movement design.

prompt: Which combination best describes 'visual comfort' in WELL Light?

options.text: Appropriate illuminance, glare control, and color quality for tasks and spaces
Maximum brightness everywhere with no shading
Elimination of all windows
Use of a single light level for 24 hours without variation

explanation: Visual comfort balances sufficient light for tasks with control of glare and quality of light spectrum, supporting occupant performance and satisfaction.

distractorExplanations.b: Excessive uncontrolled brightness increases glare.
distractorExplanations.c: Windows often support daylight and visual connection.
distractorExplanations.d: Static lighting ignores task and circadian needs.

prompt: Underfloor air distribution (UFAD) systems can create thermal stratification. What should designers monitor for seated occupant comfort?

options.text: Temperature and air speed at breathing zone and ankle level
Only rooftop PAR for landscaping
Community survey participation rates only
WELL exam scaled score distribution

explanation: UFAD can produce vertical temperature gradients; comfort assessment checks conditions at occupant head and foot levels per thermal comfort guidance.

distractorExplanations.b: Landscape photosynthetically active radiation is unrelated to indoor UFAD.
distractorExplanations.c: Community surveys do not replace environmental measurements.
distractorExplanations.d: Exam statistics are unrelated to HVAC stratification.

prompt: Which metric is used to describe how much of the year a space exceeds useful daylight illuminance thresholds?

options.text: Annual sunlight exposure or related cumulative daylight metrics used with sDA/ASE frameworks
Gallons per minute at lavatories
Occupant load for assembly spaces only
WELL feature point submittal count

explanation: Daylight analysis often pairs spatial daylight autonomy with annual sunlight exposure to avoid both insufficient daylight and excessive glare/overheating.

distractorExplanations.b: Flow rate is a Water fixture metric.
distractorExplanations.c: Assembly occupant load is an egress calculation.
distractorExplanations.d: Submittal counts are administrative, not simulation metrics.

prompt: A building provides height-adjustable desks by default rather than by request only. Which WELL principle does this default-support approach reflect?

options.text: Making active workstations accessible to all rather than treating movement as an exception
Eliminating all seated work permanently
Reducing nourishment transparency
Avoiding all thermal comfort standards

explanation: Universal provision of sit-stand desks normalizes movement during the workday and aligns with WELL Movement inclusion principles.

distractorExplanations.b: Sit-stand desks still allow seated work; they add standing option.
distractorExplanations.c: Nourishment transparency is unrelated to desk type.
distractorExplanations.d: Thermal standards still apply regardless of desk adjustability.

prompt: Which operational practice helps maintain thermal comfort after a major HVAC retrofit?

options.text: Commission controls, verify setpoints and schedules, and gather occupant feedback during seasonal changeover
Never adjust BAS schedules after installation
Disable all sensors to reduce maintenance
Ignore seasonal clothing changes

explanation: Post-retrofit commissioning and seasonal tune-up with occupant feedback helps ensure environmental systems deliver intended thermal comfort performance.

distractorExplanations.b: Schedules and setpoints often need refinement after occupancy changes.
distractorExplanations.c: Disabled sensors prevent corrective control.
distractorExplanations.d: Clothing seasonality affects perceived comfort and should inform setpoint strategy.

prompt: Which light exposure pattern most risks circadian misalignment for office workers?

options.text: Dim indoor days with very bright screens at night and little morning daylight
Consistent day-night contrast with morning daylight and dimmer evenings
Regular outdoor breaks during lunch in daylight
Perimeter desks with controlled daylight and shades

explanation: Low daytime circadian stimulus combined with high evening light from screens disrupts natural day-night signals, a key WELL Light concern.

distractorExplanations.b: Healthy contrast supports entrainment.
distractorExplanations.c: Outdoor lunch breaks increase beneficial daylight exposure.
distractorExplanations.d: Managed perimeter daylight is generally positive for visual and circadian health.

prompt: A project targets WELL Thermal Comfort verification. Which documentation type is most relevant?

options.text: HVAC design narratives, control sequences, and measurement or survey records demonstrating comfort criteria
Only marketing brochures without technical data
Unrelated food menu photos only
Exam registration receipt for WELL AP candidates

explanation: Thermal Comfort features require evidence that environmental conditions and controls meet WELL criteria through design documentation and/or performance verification.

distractorExplanations.b: Marketing materials alone lack verifiable performance data.
distractorExplanations.c: Food menus relate to Nourishment, not thermal verification.
distractorExplanations.d: Individual exam registration is not project compliance documentation.

prompt: Which integrated design decision simultaneously supports WELL Light and Thermal Comfort at the building envelope?

options.text: High-performance glazing with spectrally selective properties and exterior shading tuned by orientation
Eliminating all windows on every facade
Using only incandescent lamps with no controls
Removing all insulation to increase daylight

explanation: Spectrally selective glazing and orientation-specific shading admit useful daylight while limiting heat gain and glare, benefiting both concept areas.

distractorExplanations.b: No windows eliminates daylight benefits.
distractorExplanations.c: Incandescent-only schemes lack efficient circadian-tunable design.
distractorExplanations.d: Removing insulation worsens thermal comfort and energy performance.
